title: Warren Hastings
provisions: Founded the Calcutta Madrasah (1781) for the study of Muslim law and related subjects.
Abolished Dual system of government in bengal.

title: Lord Cornwallis
provisions: Creator of the modern civil services in India.
Banned private trade by Company officials.
Charter Act of 1793: Posts above £500 reserved for British.
Created the post of Superintendent of Police (SP).
Created District Courts and City Courts.
Created four Provincial Courts of Circuit and Appeal.
First person to Codify Laws in Bengal.

title: Jonathan Duncan
provisions: Established the Sanskrit College at Banaras (1791).

title: Lord Wellesley
provisions: Established a Civil Servants' Training College at Fort William, Calcutta.

title: John Adam
provisions: Printing press/property of defaulting printers and publishers could be confiscated for printing anything against the British Government.

title: Lord William Bentinck
provisions: Abolished the Office of SP and transferred duties to the District Magistrate.
Abolished Provincial Courts of Circuit and Appeal.

title: Thomas Babington Macaulay
provisions: Recommended English as the official language.
Recommended that Persian be replaced by English.

title: Charles Metcalfe
provisions: Removed major restrictions on the press (1835).
Known as the 'Liberator of the Indian Press'.

title: Queen Victoria
provisions: Led to the passage of the Age of Consent Act (1891).

title: Lord Canning
provisions: Appointed Maharaja of Patiala, Sir Dinkar Rao, and Raja of Banaras as non-official members of the Viceroy’s Legislative Council (1862).

title: Charles Wood
provisions: Issued Wood's Dispatch (1854) — called the 'Magna Carta of Education in India'.
Recommended vernacular language for primary education.
Led to the creation of University of Bombay, Madras, Calcutta, Thompson college, Pusa Institiute.

title: Lord Lytton
provisions: Introduced the Vernacular Press Act (1878) — called the 'Gagging Act'.

title: Lord Ripon
provisions: Enactment of the First Factory Act.
Appointment of Hunter Commission to suggest education reforms
Invasion of Afghanistan from British India

title: Lieutenant Ayerst
provisions: Assassinated along with W.C. Rand by the Chapekar Brothers.

title: Lord Curzon
provisions: Carried out the Partition of Bengal (1905).
Led to the Indian Universities Act (1904).

title: Thomas Raleigh
provisions: Led the committee on higher education reform under Lord Curzon.
Led to the Indian Universities Act (1904).

title: Lord Minto II
provisions: Facilitated the creation of the All India Muslim League (1906).
Received a deputation led by the Aga Khan (Shimla Deputation, 1906).

title: Douglas Kingsford
provisions: Target of an assassination attempt by Prafulla Chaki and Khudiram Bose.

title: Valentine Chirol
provisions: Called Tilak the 'Father of Indian Unrest'.
Tilak filed a defamation case against him.

title: Michael O'Dwyer
provisions: In charge of Punjab during the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre period.

title: Sadler Commission
provisions: Appointed to review the Calcutta University.
Recommended 12 years of schooling before a degree course.

title: Montagu
provisions: Issued a statement leading to the release of Annie Besant.
These led to the fading of the Home Rule Movement.

title: Sidney Rowlatt
provisions: The Rowlatt Act was named after him.
Intended to curtail terrorist violence but was regarded as draconian.

title: General Dyer
provisions: Ordered firing on the unarmed crowd at Jallianwala Bagh (13 April 1919).

title: Rudyard Kipling
provisions: Supported General Dyer's actions at Jallianwala Bagh.

title: C.F. Andrews
provisions: British Clergyman and Associate of Gandhi, c. 1919.
Termed the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre as 'one of the most heinous crimes against humanity'.

title: Hunter Commission
provisions: Constituted to inquire into the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre.

title: Lord Reading
provisions: Failed talks with Gandhi (facilitated by Madan Mohan Malaviya) during the Non-Cooperation Movement.

title: Lee Commission
provisions: Recommended early review of the Government of India Act, 1919.

title: Alexander Muddiman
provisions: Recommended the creation of the Indian Statutory Commission.

title: Charles Tegart
provisions: Target of an assassination attempt by Gopinath Saha (1924).

title: Lord Birkenhead
provisions: Challenged Indian leaders to draft their own constitutional scheme.

title: Stanley Baldwin
provisions: Announced the formation of the Simon Commission.

title: John Simon
provisions: Led the all-white Simon Commission to review the constitutional scenario.

title: Stanley Jackson
provisions: Target of an assassination attempt by Bina Das.

title: John Saunders
provisions: Killed by Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev (1928).
To avenge the death of Lala Lajpat Rai.

title: Hartog Commission
provisions: Focused on efficiency and quality of existing institutions.

title: Ramsay MacDonald
provisions: Provided separate electorates for the Depressed Classes.
Led to the Poona Pact between Gandhi and Ambedkar.

title: Charles Stevens
provisions: Killed by Shanti Ghosh and Suniti Choudhury (1930).

title: N.S. Simpson
provisions: Killed in the Writers' Building Attack by Binoy-Badal-Dinesh.

title: Lord Willingdon
provisions: Suppressed the resumed Civil Disobedience Movement.

title: Lord Linlithgow
provisions: Announced the August Offer (1940) during WWII.

title: Winston Churchill
provisions: Criticised Gandhi as a 'half-naked seditious fakir'.

title: Sir Stafford Cripps
provisions: Leader of Cripps Mission, 1942 and Member of Cabinet Mission, 1946.
Led the Cripps Mission (1942) with Dominion Status proposals.

title: Clement Attlee
provisions: Declared that power would be transferred by June 1948.

title: Lord Pethick-Lawrence
provisions: Cabinet Mission Member and Secretary of State, 1945–1947.

title: Lord Louis Mountbatten
provisions: 3 June Plan (Mountbatten Plan) — creation of two Dominions (India and Pakistan).

title: Sir Cyril Radcliffe
provisions: Established the boundary between India and Pakistan.
